posts.author: Carlos-i (Carlos Garcia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
I am trying to upload an image to an update entry, but I get the next message
UserUnauthroizedException",“status_code”:403,“error_message”:“User unauthorized to perform action”,“error_data”

I found a previous topic with the same problem but the account has never lost his admin privileges, my user can write on the board, actually the script created the updates inside the board, and several items and subitems, before trying to upload the image. I have been using the API v2 token.

Any help is greatly appreciated.

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Carlos-i

That sounds quite odd! Would you be able to reach out to us via email so that we could troubleshoot further?

I’d love to see the specific query you are using leading up to the issue, including a timestamp of the last error received. This would help us troubleshoot the behavior further and hopefully, find a solution for it.

I’ve reached out to you via DM I’m looking forward to hearing from you soon!

-Alex

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Alex is OOO next week so he won’t respond to the DM… I slacked him to ask if he can add us to the DM. If not, let’s open a ticket with this guy. I’ll send his email over slack

posts.author: Carlos-i (Carlos Garcia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Alex

Sure I will, Thank you in advance

Carlos

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done

posts.author: Helen (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml:
To all future readers:

At this time we have been able to identify a fix for this issue! It was simply a case of mixed up API keys.
